overview.short: Amboli is a small village in the southern Konkan Division, Sindhudurg district, Maharashtra state, India.
overview.description: Amboli is a small village in the southern Konkan Division, Sindhudurg district, Maharashtra state, India. Amboli Ghat lies at an altitude of approximately 700 metres (2,300 feet) above sea level and is the last hill resort before ending in the coastal plains of Goa state. Amboli lies in the Western Ghats of peninsular India, one of the world's "Ecological Hotspots" and it abounds in unusual flora and fauna. However, as in the other parts of the Sahaydri Hills, denudation of the forest cover and unregulated government-assisted development are gradually ruining a once-pristine environment.

topPlaces.name: Hiranyakeshi river
topPlaces.description: The Hiranyakeshi river is a left-bank tributary of the Ghataprabha River originating in the Western Ghats in the Sindhudurg district of Maharashtra.

topPlaces.name: Chaukul
topPlaces.description: Chaukul Village is located near Sawantwadi taluka of Sindhudurg district, Maharashtra State in India. The village belongs to Konkan Division.

topPlaces.name: Kalambist
topPlaces.description: Kalmbist is a village in the Sindhudurg district of the Konkan division of the Indian state of Maharashtra.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Amboli?
faq.a: The best time to visit Amboli is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

The nearest airport is Manohar International Airport, Mopa (North Goa) (29 km) and the nearest railway station is Vasco da Gama (Goa) (66 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Amboli?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Amboli at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Amboli?
faq.a: Stays in Amboli start from ₹1,200 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Amboli?
faq.a: The top attractions are Hiranyakeshi river, Chaukul, Kalambist.
